<result>Perfect! I have gathered comprehensive information. Let me create a final summary report:

## Comprehensive Deferred Items Report - CipherBox Phases 11-17.1

I've completed a thorough search through all 22 phase directories in `.planning/milestones/m2/phases/` across CONTEXT.md, PLAN.md, VERIFICATION.md, SUMMARY.md, and RESEARCH.md files. Here are all deferred, postponed, and future work items:

---

### **Phase 11: Windows Desktop**

**Deferred to Phase 11.3:**
- Linux Desktop support (now a separate phase rather than bundled)
- ProjFS as potential future Windows alternative if Rust ecosystem matures

---

### **Phase 11.1: macOS Desktop Catch-Up**

**Deferred to Phase 11.2:**
- `approveDevice()` UI trigger — function is API-complete but has no UI (auth.ts line 674, main.ts line 31)
- Approval listener for device approval responses

**Deferred to Phase 11 (cross-platform):**
- WalletConnect QR code flow — currently only injected provider MVP; full WalletConnect deferred

---

### **Phase 12: Multi-Factor Authentication (Core Kit Identity Provider)**

**Deferred to Phase 12.3 (SIWE):**
- External wallet login (removed from active Phase 12 flow)
- Method linking UI in LinkedMethods component
- Web3Auth wallet connector support

**Deferred to Plan 04:**
- User display/info from PnP (currently removed; user info will display in Plan 04)

---

### **Phase 12.1: AES-CTR Streaming Encryption**

**Deferred to future gap-closing phase:**
- Desktop FUSE CTR support — web app gets CTR playback; desktop client update deferred to separate phase addressing all Phase 12.x macOS gaps

**Deferred (out of scope):**
- BYO-IPFS configuration f

**Key Files for Reference:**
- `/Users/michael/Code/cipher-box/.planning/milestones/m2/phases/[phase]/[phase]-CONTEXT.md` — Primary source for all deferred items under `<deferred>` tags
- `/Users/michael/Code/cipher-box/.planning/milestones/m2/phases/[phase]/[phase]-VERIFICATION.md` — Some deferred verification items
- `/Users/michael/Code/cipher-box/.planning/milestones/m2/phases/[phase]/[phase]-SUMMARY.md` — Plan-specific deferrals documented inline</result>

task:
## Summary Statistics

- **Total phases analyzed:** 14 unique phases (plus sub-phases)
- **Phases with deferred items:** 11 out of 14
- **Total deferred/postponed items:** 40+ distinct items
- **Most common deferral targets:** 
  - Phases 12.3-12.4 (auth/MFA enhancements)
  - Phase 14 (sharing-related features)
  - Milestone 4 (offline queue, merge logic)
  - Future undefined phases (desktop updates, gap closures)
user_feedback_text:
## Summary Statistics

- **Total phases analyzed:** 14 unique phases (plus sub-phases)
- **Phases with deferred items:** 11 out of 14
- **Total deferred/postponed items:** 40+ distinct items
- **Most common deferral targets:** 
  - Phases 12.3-12.4 (auth/MFA enhancements)
  - Phase 14 (sharing-related features)
  - Milestone 4 (offline queue, merge logic)
  - Future undefined phases (desktop updates, gap closures)

task:
### **Phase 11.4: Cross-Platform E2E Testing**

**Deferred ideas:**
- None — discussion stayed within phase scope

### **Phase 11.3: Linux Desktop**

**Deferred ideas:**
- None — discussion stayed within phase scope

### **Phase 17: Recycle Bin**

**Deferred ideas:**
- User-configurable retention period per-user settings
- Organization-level retention policy
- Desktop .Trash folder integration (Finder/Explorer native trash)
- Desktop bin browsing and restore UI
- Folder-hierarchy view in bin (group by original path)

**Deferred/known limitation:**
- TEE unenrollment on permanent delete (if API endpoint exists later; currently deferred to "Phase 14 TODO") — at minimum, orphaned IPNS records will expire naturally via 24-hour IPNS record lifetime; TEE republish service has capacity warnings at 1000+ records

### **Phase 16: Advanced Sync**

**Rescoped from original roadmap (deferred to Milestone 4):**
- Offline operation queue — persist write operations in IndexedDB for replay on reconnect
- Idempotent replay — idempotency keys for queued operations to prevent duplicates (tightly coupled to offline queue)
- Auto-merge of non-conflicting folder changes — three-way merge on encrypted folder metadata arrays (high complexity)
- Per-file IPNS conflict detection — currently covered by versioning safety net; re-evaluate in M4

**Deferred implementation details:**
- OS notification on conflict (CONTEXT.md calls for it but AppHandle not easily accessible from background thread; tray status change remains visible)

### **Phase 15.1: Client-Side Search**

**Deferred ideas:**
- Recent search history (show 2-3 past queries) — nice-to-have for future release
- Searching shared items — would require indexing across share boundaries (separate phase)
- Full-text content search — fundamentally different (requires decrypting file contents); out of scope

### **Phase 15: Link Sharing**

**Out of scope:**
- Unauthenticated file viewing (would be fundamentally different security surface)
- Password-protected links
- Multi-claim links (maxClaims column ready; just needs UI)
- Short domain URLs (requires DNS/redirect infrastructure)

**Deferred to Phase 15.1:**
- Client-side search (split into separate phase)
user_feedback_text:
### **Phase 15: Link Sharing**

**Out of scope:**
- Unauthenticated file viewing (would be fundamentally different security surface)
- Password-protected links
- Multi-claim links (maxClaims column ready; just needs UI)
- Short domain URLs (requires DNS/redirect infrastructure)

**Deferred to Phase 15.1:**
- Client-side search (split into separate phase)

task:
### **Phase 14: User-to-User Sharing**

**Out of scope (this phase):**
- Read-write sharing (read-only only; requires giving recipients IPNS private key)
- Re-sharing (readers receive decryption keys only, not IPNS signing keys)

**Deferred ideas:**
- User discovery/lookup service by email, username, or wallet address (requires privacy controls; separate phase)
- Display names for recipients instead of truncated pubkeys (depends on user discovery/profile phase)
- Immediate key rotation on revoke (currently lazy rotation; easy to upgrade later)
- Share notifications for recipients (currently relies on polling)
- CRDT-based IPNS inbox approach (promising third option pending IPNS reliability research) — documented in `.planning/todos/pending/2026-02-22-crdt-ipns-inbox-sharing.md`

### **Phase 13: File Versioning**

**Deferred ideas:**
- None explicitly listed; discussion stayed within phase scope

### **Phase 12.6: Per-File IPNS Metadata Split**

**Deferred to Phase 14:**
- Two TODO comments in folder.service.ts for TEE unenrollment on file deletion and orphaned TEE enrollment cleanup — marked as intentionally deferred; orphaned enrollments expire naturally via 24-hour IPNS record lifetime

**Deferred to later phase or quick task:**
- Desktop FUSE client v2 metadata support — desktop client has zero awareness of per-file IPNS records; web app gets v2 first; desktop catches up in follow-up phase or quick task

**Deferred ideas:**
- Dedicated single-file publish endpoint (useful for Phase 14 per-file sharing)
- TEE enrollment drift reconciliation — periodic full-vault scan to sync enrollment with actual vault contents (add if drift appears in production)

### **Phase 12.2: Encrypted Device Registry**

**Deferred ideas (acknowledged for future):**

- **M1 (Medium):** `itemName` stored plaintext on server — encrypt with recipient's public key (ECIES); decrypt client-side

task:
### Pending TODO Items (in `.planning/todos/pending/`)

These are explicitly tracked as deferred work items:

1. **2026-02-07: Offload large file encryption to Web Worker**
   - Files >= 10MB should use Web Worker; currently blocks main thread
   - Three options documented: event loop yielding (quick win), full Web Worker (best), incremental updates (ideal long-term)

2. **2026-02-14: Add ERC-1271 contract wallet authentication support**
   - Smart contract wallets (Safe, Argent, Sequence) need ERC-1271 `isValidSignature` verification
   - Requires on-chain call to wallet contract; needs RPC endpoint decision (free/paid/user-provided)

3. **2026-02-22: Research CRDT-based IPNS inbox for serverless share discovery**
   - Decentralized share discovery via CRDTs over IPNS, replacing centralized `shares` table
   - Addresses write-conflict issues as horizontal concern; applies to folder metadata sync, device registry, share inbox
   - Known challenges: write access control, IPNS reliability, revocation, state size growth

4. **2026-02-24: Make search index build async/incremental for large vaults**
   - `buildFromFolderTree()` currently synchronous; can block UI for large vaults
   - Three options: event loop yielding (quick win), Web Worker (better), incremental updates (ideal)

5. **2026-02-26: Add alternative MFA factor types**
   - Currently only device shares + recovery mnemonic
   - Priority: Passkey (WebAuthn PRF, best but fragmented platform support) > Password-derived key (simplest, weaker security) > Secondary OAuth (moderate complexity)
   - Not viable: TOTP, SMS/Email OTP (ephemeral, can't derive deterministic keys)
   - Deferred: Social recovery (high complexity, Shamir Secret Sharing among contacts)

6. **2026-03-23: Investigate removal of mock-ipns-routing layer**
   - Was needed for local dev; now that someguy at `192.168.133.114:8190` works, mock layer may be unnecessary
   - Requires verification someguy works reliably for all IPNS scenarios; if so, re

## CIPHERBOX FEATURE SET MAPPING

### WEB PLATFORM (React + TypeScript)

#### Authentication Features
- **Login Methods**
  - Google OAuth (via Auth0)

  - Wallet login (via Web3Auth + SIWE - Sign In With Ethereum)
  - Test-only login (via `/auth/test-login`)
- **Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)**
  - MFA enrollment/enablement via Web3Auth DKG
  - Recovery phrase generation (24-word BIP39)
  - Device approval flow (new device MFA challenge)
  - Device waiting screen (pending approval from existing device)
  - Recovery input fallback (when approval not available)
  - Authorized devices list (view previously approved devices)
- **Auth Management**
  - Link multiple auth methods to single account
  - Unlink auth methods (minimum one required)
  - Logout with token revocation
  - Account deletion (permanent)
- **Device Management**
  - Device identity tracking via device ID and fingerprint
  - Device registry sync across instances
  - ECIES ephemeral key exchange for device approval

#### File Operations
- **File Upload**
  - Single file upload via modal dialog
  - Drag-and-drop upload zone
  - Concurrent upload handling
  - Upload progress tracking
  - Error recovery with retry
  - File size validation (max 100MB)
  - Storage quota enforcement (500MB limit)
  - Automatic file encryption before upload
- **File Download**
  - Single file download with decryption
  - Batch fil

### E2E TEST COVERAGE

#### Test Files (12 test suites)
1. **full-workflow.spec.ts** - Complete user journey with nested folders, file operations, editing
2. **sharing-workflow.spec.ts** - User-to-user sharing (read-only and multi-recipient)
3. **writable-shares.spec.ts** - Write-permission sharing and recipient mutations
4. **invite-link-workflow.spec.ts** - Public invite links and claiming
5. **recycle-bin.spec.ts** - Delete, restore, permanent delete, empty bin
6. **search-workflow.spec.ts** - Search palette with keyboard/click navigation
7. **mfa-flows.spec.ts** - MFA enrollment, device approval, recovery phrase
8. **wallet-login.spec.ts** - Wallet (EIP-6963 via mock) login
9. **recovery.spec.ts** - Vault recovery tool (independent recovery)
10. **conflict-detection.spec.ts** - Multi-device conflict detection and auto-resync
11. **journey-timing.spec.ts** - Performance timing benchmarks
12. **load-test.spec.ts** - Concurrent operation stress testing

#### Feature Coverage by Test
- **Authentication**: MFA flows, wallet login, email OTP, recovery
- **File Operations**: Upload, download, rename, delete, move, edit, replace, preview
- **Folder Operations**: Create, navigate, rename, move, delete
- **Sharing**: Direct shares, invite links, permissions, revocation, hiding
- **Bin**: Soft delete, restore, permanent delete, empty
- **Search**: Query, navigation, keyboard/UI activation
- **Conflicts**: Multi-device scenarios, sequence number conflicts
- **Media**: Image preview, PDF viewer, video player, audio player

### SDK (TypeScript Package)

#### Core Client (`CipherBoxClient`)
- **Initialization**
  - Constructor with vault keypair, root folder key, API config
  - Event emitter registration
  - Pinning provider configuration (Kubo, Pinata, PSA)
- **Folder Operations**
  - `loadFolder(ipnsName)` - Load and decrypt folder metadata
  - `createFolder(parentIpnsName, name)` - Create new folder
  - `renameItem(ipnsName, childId, newName)` - Rename file/folder
  - `moveItem(sourceIpnsName, destIpnsName, childId)` - Move to different folder
  - `deleteItem(ipnsName, childId)` - Delete item
- **File Operations**
  - `uploadFile(ipnsName, content, fileName, mimeType, progressFn)` - Upload with progress callback
  - `downloadFile(ipnsName, childId)` - Download and decrypt
  - `downloadFromIpns(ipnsName, fileKey)` - Direct IPNS download
- **Bin Operations**
  - `loadBin()` - Load recycle bin entries
  - `deleteToBin(ipnsName, childId, path)` - Soft delete
  - `restoreFromBin(entryId, targetIpnsName)` - Restore item
  - `permanentDelete(entryId)` - Hard delete
  - `emptyBin()` - Delete all bin entries
- **Share Operations**
  - `shareFolder(ipnsName, recipientPublicKey, permission)` - Create share
  - `revokeShare(shareId)` - Revoke share
  - `reWrapForRecipients(shares, newItems)` - Re-wrap keys for share recipients
- **Lifecycle**
  - `destroy()` - Clear all key material from memory
  - `hasFolder(ipnsName)` - Check if folder loaded
  - `on(handler)` - Subscribe to events
  - `off(handler)` - Unsubscribe

#### State Management
- **FolderTree** - In-memory folder hierarchy with sequence numbers
- **KeyCache** - Cache for decrypted folder/file keys
- **Event System** - Type-safe event emission (operation start/end/error)

#### Cryptographic Functions
- Folder metadata encryption/decryption
- File encryption/decryption
- File metadata encryption
- Key wrapping/unwrapping (ECIES)
- HKDF key derivation
- AES-256-GCM encryption

### API BACKEND (NestJS + TypeScript)

#### Authentication Endpoints
- **POST `/auth/login`** - Authenticate with Web3Auth ID token, create/find user, issue JWT
- **POST `/auth/refresh`** - Refresh JWT using refresh token (cookie or body)
- **POST `/auth/logout`** - Invalidate all refresh tokens
- **DELETE `/auth/account`** - Permanently delete user and all data
- **GET `/auth/methods`** - List linked auth methods
- **POST `/auth/link`** - Link new auth method to account
- **POST `/auth/unlink`** - Unlink auth method
- **POST `/auth/test-login`** - Test-only login (requires TEST_LOGIN_SECRET)

#### Identity Endpoints (via `identity.controller.ts`)
- **GET `/auth/identity/wallet/nonce`** - SIWE nonce for wallet login
- **POST `/auth/identity/wallet`** - SIWE verification and login
- **POST `/auth/identity/email/send-otp`** - Send OTP to email
- **POST `/auth/identity/email/verify-otp`** - Verify OTP and login
- **POST `/auth/identity/google`** - Google OAuth login

#### Vault Endpoints
- **POST `/vault/init`** - Initialize user vault with encrypted keys
- **GET `/vault`** - Retrieve user vault
- **GET `/vault/config`** - Get vault configuration (retention periods, etc.)
- **GET `/vault/export`** - Export vault for independent recovery
- **GET `/vault/quota`** - Get storage usage and limits

#### File/IPFS Endpoints
- **POST `/ipfs/upload`** - Upload encrypted file (multipart form-data, max 100MB)
- **POST `/ipfs/unpin`** - Unpin files (stop tracking quota)
- **POST `/ipfs/register-cid`** - Register CID for quota tracking

#### IPNS Endpoints
- **POST `/ipns/publish`** - Publish single IPNS record (pre-signed by client)
- **POST `/ipns/publish-batch`** - Batch publish up to 200 IPNS records
- **GET `/ipns/resolve`** - Resolve IPNS name to CID

#### Share Endpoints
- **POST `/shares`** - Create share (user-to-user)
- **GET `/shares/received`** - List received shares (paginated)
- **GET `/shares/sent`** - List sent shares (paginated)
- **GET `/shares/lookup`** - Lookup

### DESKTOP PLATFORM (Tauri + Rust)

#### File System Mount
- **FUSE-T Mount (macOS)**
  - SMB backend (not NFS) - works around macOS kernel bug
  - Mount at `~/CipherBox`
  - Pre-population of root folder from IPNS
  - Pre-population of subfolders (recursive with caching)
  - Inode table with stable inode numbering
  - Metadata cache with TTL
  - Content cache with prefetch
- **FUSE Operations**
  - `lookup()` - find inode by name
  - `getattr()` - file/folder attributes
  - `readdir()` - list directory contents
  - `read()` - file content with async prefetch
  - `write()` - buffered writes to temp file
  - `create()` - new files
  - `mkdir()` - new folders
  - `rename()` - move/rename items
  - `unlink()` - delete files
  - `rmdir()` - delete folders
  - `release()` - close file (trigger encrypt+upload)
  - `opendir()` / `releasedir()` - directory handles

#### File Operations via FUSE
- **Transparent File Access**
  - Read encrypted files transparently
  - Write files with encryption on close
  - Folder navigation
  - File metadata access
- **Encryption Integration**
  - Client-side encryption for all writes
  - On-demand decryption for reads
  - File key derivation and wrapping
  - File IV tracking
  - Version support
- **Upload/Publish**
  - Encrypted file upload to IPFS
  - IPNS metadata publish (debounced)
  - Sequence number tracking
  - Conflict detection

#### Desktop-Specific Features
- **Auth Management**
  - Dev-key mode (headless login for testing)
  - Test-login via `/auth/test-login`
  - Token refresh handling
  - OAuth popup window management
- **Keychain Integration**
  - Secure device ID storage
  - Private key persistence (release builds only)
  - Debug builds use ephemeral UUIDs
- **Tray Integration**
  - System tray icon
  - Status indicator
  - Mount/unmount controls
  - Quit app
- **Sync Management**
  - Background sync daemon
  - Device registry polling
  - Folder metadata refresh
  - MFA status tracking
- **Background Tasks**
  - Debounced
